gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف أو مهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 66)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف أو مهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 66)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام) يَقُولُ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى وَ لا يُؤْذَنُ لَهُمْ فَيَعْتَذِرُونَ فَقَالَ اللَّهُ أَجَلُّ وَ أَعْدَلُ وَ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ أَنْ يَكُونَ لِعَبْدِهِ عُذْرٌ لَا يَدَعُهُ يَعْتَذِرُ بِهِ وَ لَكِنَّهُ فُلِجَ فَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ عُذْرٌ.

en.hubeali: H 14648 – From him, from Ali, from Ismail Bin Mahraan, from Hammad Bin Busman who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying with regards to the Statement of Allahazwj Blessed and High: “[77:36] And permission shall not be given to them so that they should offer excuses”. So Imamasws said: ‘Allahazwj is more Majestic, and Just, and Greater than it, that if a servant has an excuse, Heazwj would not Allow it to be presented by him. But, rather (it means that) he would fail and run out of excuses’.
en.sarwar: 200. From him, from Ali, from Ismail Bin Mahraan, from Hammad Bin Busman who said, ‘I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying with regards to the Statement of Allah (azwj) Blessed and High: “[77:36] And permission shall not be given to them so that they should offer excuses”. So Imam (a.s.) said: ‘Allah (azwj) is more Majestic, and Just, and Greater than it, that if a servant has an excuse, He (azwj) would not Allow it to be presented by him. But, rather (it means that) he would fail and run out of excuses’.
